user_id.description: The [User](#User-object) ID of the owner of setting.

data.description: Raw JSON field for the settings.

type.description: Type of the `UserSetting`. Possible values are: ticket-views, user-views, preferences

User.description:
A User represents a person or a bot which contacted your team via Gorgias.
The `User` object is used for both end-users, customers, visitors and agents or admins of your Gorgias account.
From Gorgias's perspective everybody is a `User`, and they are distinguished by their [Roles](#Role-object).

lastname.description: Last name of the user. This field is read-only and generated using the name field

updated_datetime.description: When the user was last updated.

integrations.description: A list of integration data rows that this user has. For example a user can have Shopify integration data (orders, payments, etc...)

auths.description: A list of [UserAuth](#UserAuth-object) objects. Each object represents a way to connect to Gorgias. This field is read-only.

settings.description: A list of UserSetting objects. Each setting field represents a persona preference of a user.

external_id.description: External id can be used to identify this user in a foreign system. This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish.

country.description: Country of the user. We're using ISO_3166-1 (Alpha 2) codes from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_3166-1

email.description: Primary email address of the User

deactivated_datetime.description: When the user was deactivated. If this field is set, the active field will be False

timezone.description: Timezone of the user. We're using timezones defined on this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_tz_database_time_zones

firstname.description: First name of the user. This field is read-only and generated using the name field

customer.description: User defined JSON field. It can be useful for storing additional info about the object.

language.description: The preferred language of the user. We're using ISO_639-1 codes from this list: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes

roles.description: A list of Role objects. Each account has a predefined list of roles so you only have to supply the name.

signature_html.description: HTML version of the user's signature. Useful for agents mostly.

meta.description: User defined JSON field. It can be useful for storing additional info about the object.

signature_text.description: Text version of the user's signature. Useful for agents mostly.

channels.description: A list of UserChannel objects. Each channel represents a way to communicate with a user. Example: email, phone, facebook, etc..

password.description: Password - only agent and admin roles have it.

active.description: A convenience field that indicates if the user is active. This field is read-only. If you want to deactivate a user you can set the deactivated_datetime field

Rule.description:
Rules provide a way to control the behavior of Gorgias after certain events occurred.

For example you can automatically add a 'refund' Tag on a ticket if the Ticket subject contains the text 'refund',
or send a satisfaction survey after the ticket was closed for more than 48h.

Rules executed in the order of their priority field. The higher the priority, the sooner the Rule is executed.

title.description: A short description of the Rule.

user_id.description: The original author of the rule.

updated_datetime.description: When was the last time this Rule was updated.

priority.description: Order of execution. Higher priorities are executed earlier.

description.description: A long description of the Rule. Use it to give more details about a rule.
description.example: Automatically close all tickets from Amazon because there is nothing to do for them. We still want to keep them to be able to search.

code_ast.description: Abstract Syntax Tree representation of the `code` field.

event_types.description: A list of comma separated events that this rule will be executed on. This field is mutually exclusive with the `schedule` field. Do not set both.

schedule.description:
Rule execution interval. The format is similar to crontab. 
        This field is mutually exclusive with the `event_types` field. Do not set both.',
        See more details about the format here: 
        https://manpages.debian.org/jessie/cron/crontab.5.en.html#EXAMPLE_CRON_FILE

deactivated_datetime.description: When was this Rule was deactivated.

code.description: Subset of Javascript language used to execute this Rule.

type.description: A system or user rule. System rules are automatically generated and cannot be changed or deleted.

Action.description:
An action is a single operation that can be applied to a [Ticket](#Ticket-object).
        An Action can only be part of a [Macro](#Macro-object).

title.description: User defined title of the action

description.description: A longer text explaining what the action is supposed to do.

type.description: Shows if an action is user or system defined

View.description:
A View defines a way to filter a list of Tickets and Users. In the 
        customer support industry they are also known as Queues or Saved Searches.
        Technically a view has a type (ticket-list/user-list), 0 or more filters and sorting metadata.
        The language of filters we use is a subset of Javascript.

fields.description: List of columns (and their order) to be displayed in the view

type.description: Used to identify what list it should filter.

user_id.description: The original author of the view

filters.description: Javascript code used to filter the view

order_by.description: Field used to sort the view.

display_order.description:
Default display order of the view (higher first). 
                       This is the order which the user sees the first time they open the helpdesk - 
                       they can then re-order their views.

slug.description: (Obsolete) Used to display a nice URL for the view. This field will be removed soon.

order_dir.description: Sorting direction of the view. Can be either `asc` or `desc`.

filters_ast.description: Abstract Syntax Tree representation of the filters

group_by.description: (Obsolete) What field to use to group the view data by. This field will be removed soon.

name.description: Name/Title of the view. Displayed for all users

decoration.description: Used to decorate the view with a special color or icons.

category.description:
Category is an internal field used to identify system and user views.
                       Example of system views: Trash or Spam. System views cannot be deleted.

Macro.description:
A macro (aka canned responses) is a collection of [Action](#Action-object) objects that can 
        be applied on a ticket.

For example a macro can set the body text and add a [Tag](#Tag-object) to a [Ticket](Ticket-object).
In this case a macro would have 2 Actions: A `setBodyText` action and a `addTag` action.

usage.description: How many times this macro was used on tickets. Useful for sorting the most used macros first.

name.description: The name of the Macro. Suggestion: choose a name that can be easily searched

updated_datetime.description: When the Macro was last updated.

external_id.description: External id can be used to identify this macro in a foreign system. Not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it to your own value.

actions.description: A list of [Action](#Action-object) objects that are to be executed when the Macro is used.

TicketMessage.description:
A `TicketMessage` is an incoming or outgoing message. 
A `TicketMessage` has one and only one [Ticket](#Ticket-object) parent.

A ticket message can represent an email, phone call, facebook post or a web chat message.
All of those types of messages are unified into a single format of the TicketMessage.

**Note**: Be careful when creating `TicketMessage` because they can be actually sent to the customer.
Make sure `sent_datetime` is set if you don't want to notify customers.

sender.description: The User object who sent this message.

via.description: Indicates from which origin the ticket has been created. For example, it can be `api`, `helpdesk`, `user_form`....

opened_datetime.description: When the message was opened. Useful to know if the customer has opened/seen the message.

from_agent.description: Indicates whether this message was sent by an agent or by a user.

headers.description: Email headers of the message (if the message is an email).

body_html.example:
Hello,

I can't place an order on your site, it says: I don't have enough credit.
How can I add some credits?

Cheers,
John Doe
body_html.description: The full HTML version of the body of this message, if there is one.

stripped_text.description: The stripped text version of the body of this message. Stripped signature, previous replies, etc..

failed_datetime.description: If the message sending failed this field will be set with the datetime when it happened. Failed messages can usually be retried.

source.description: A JSON field which stores all data related to who sent this message, through which channel, and to whom. This is necessary in addition of the sender/channel/receiver field because this is immutable: even if the sender changes his email address, the address he used to send this message originally will still be stored here.

message_id.description: Id of the message on the service responsible for sending the message.It can be the id of an email, a Messenger message, etc...

macros.description: An array of macros applied to this message.

stripped_signature.description: *Obsolete field. Will be removed in the future*

rule_id.description: Id of the rule which sent this message, if any.

attachments.description: An array of files attached to this message.

integration_id.description: Id of the integration with which this message has been received or sent.

actions.description: An array of external actions executed with this message. These actions are set by macros.

body_text.example:
Hello,

        I can't place an order on your site, it says: I don't have enough credit.
        How can I add some credits?

        Cheers,
        John Doe
body_text.description: The full text version of the body of this message, if there is one.

channel.description: The channel used to send this message.

ticket_id.description: The ID of the ticket this message is associated with.

meta.description: User defined JSON field. It can be useful for storing additional info about the object.

public.description: Indicates whether this message was sent to the user, or is an internal note.

receiver.description: The User who received this message.

external_id.description: Another ID for the message. Can be set freely.

sent_datetime.description: When the message was actually sent from Gorgias. Because message sending is async, Gorgias will update this field once the message was processed and delivered via it's corresponding integration.

stripped_html.description: The stripped HTML version of the body of this message, if there is one. Stripped signature, previous replies, etc..

Request.description:
A `Request` is used to mark a [Tickets](#Ticket-object) with the last query that the customer had. 
They are mostly used in (Rules)[#Rule-object] to perform automatic responses.

name.description: Name of the request. Keep it short and as generic as possible.

decoration.description: A JSON field that is used to decorate the request with color and/or and icon.

Source.description:
A Source is an object that describes the addresses/names used to communicate with a customer 
        in a context of a TicketMessage. The Source object populates the `TicketMessage.source` field.

bcc.description: A list of BCC addresses. Only present in an email context.

cc.description: A list of CC addresses. Only present in an email context.

to.description: A list of To addresses. To and From are usually present for most channel types.

extra.description: Contains extra data about where the message comes from (i.e. the smooch_app_id)

from.description: A single From address. To and From are usually present for most channel types.

type.description:
(Obsolete) 
        Describes a more detailed channel information of how the message was sent/received. 
        This field will be removed soon.

Widget.description:
A Widget is a container/card that is displayed in the right-hand sidebar of the ticket or user page. 
        It contains a list of fields and values that come Integrations.

order.description: Order in which widgets are displayed. Lowest first.

template.description: Template to use to render the data that comes into the widget

context.description: In which context to display this widget. Possible values are user or ticket.

updated_datetime.description: When the Widget was last updated.

type.description: To which kind of data this widget is attached (the type of the widget is used for rendering inside Gorgias). Possible values are custom, shopify, stripe, http, recharge, smooch_inside

integration_id.description:
Integration that this widget's data is attached to. Only used for widgets attached to HTTP 
integrations. Widgets are the same for all Shopify integrations, but each HTTP integration need a specific widget.

IntegrationHTTP.description: (Obsolete) - This object will be removed soon. Please do not use it.

IntegrationFacebook.description: (Obsolete) - This object will be removed soon. Please do not use it.

IntegrationMapping.description: (Obsolete) - This object will be removed soon. Please do not use it.

Integration.description:
Gorgias is a lot more useful if connected with external services such as Gmail, Shopify, Chat systems, etc..
This is where Integrations come in. They are a way to connect with these external services.

There are official Gorgias integrations that can be found in the Integrations page in the helpdesk and there are
custom HTTP integrations. Most integrations are using a HTTP API.

mappings.description: *Obsolete field! Will be removed in the future.*

updated_datetime.description: When the Integration was last updated.

facebook.description: *Obsolete field! Will be removed in the future.*

connections.description: Array of [UserConnection](#UserConnection-object) objects attached to this integration. A connection provides a way to connect to an external service.

type.description: Type of the integration. Possible values are aircall, zendesk, http, smooch, smooch_inside, facebook, shopify, email, gmail, recharge.

locked_datetime.description: When the Integration was locked. Locking an integration is done during a big sync operation, for example.

meta.description: User defined JSON field. It can be useful for storing additional info about the object.

name.description: Name of the integration. Usually the email address, facebook page name, etc..

http.description: *Obsolete field! Will be removed in the future.*

deactivated_datetime.description: When the Integration was deactivated. If not set, means that the integration is active.

decoration.description: Contains various data about how to display the integration in Gorgias. This field is not currently used.

Role.description:
Roles are used to distinguish between different types of users.
Technically a Role is a group of users that have the same permissions. By default all Gorgias accounts have 3 roles: 
admin, agent and user.

Currently no custom roles are allowed. Please contact us if you need more roles for your application.

name.description: Name of the role. Possible values are: user, agent and admin. Currently no custom roles are allowed.

updated_datetime.description: When the role was last updated.

external_id.description: External id can be used to identify this role in a foreign system.This field is not used by Gorgias, feel free to set it as you wish.

Event.description:
An `Event` is used to let you know if anything happened on your [Account](#Account-object).
For example: if a [user](#User-object) was created there will be an [Event](#Event-object) object with the 
type: "user-created".

Events are mostly used for internal purposes (ticket-created, ticket-updated, etc..), but you can create your own 
custom events and store them in Gorgias.

data.description: Raw JSON data of the event

object_type.description: In combination with `object_id` it's used to reference an existing object in Gorgias (Ticket, User, etc..)

object_id.description: In combination with `object_type` it's used to reference an existing object in Gorgias

context.description: Usually an uuid4 used to group a sequence of events together.

user_id.description: User ID of the Owner/Author of the object

type.description: Event types are used to discriminate between events.There are certain system-reserved event types.

Account.description: Your company's account object on Gorgias. Here you'll find all your account's settings.

user_id.description: User ID of the Owner/Author of the object

updated_datetime.description: When the Account was last updated.

status.description: Current status of the account (usually related to the state of the [current_subscription](#Account-object-current_subscription) field).

meta.description: User defined JSON field. It can be useful for storing additional info about the object.

items.description: Represents a subscription to a Stripe plan

deactivated_datetime.description: When the Account was deactivated. If not set, means that the Account is active.

Ticket.description:
A `Ticket` is a conversation between your team (Ex: admins/agents/bots) and 
your users (Ex: customers/prospects). We think of tickets like email threads or chat conversations.
Tickets have multiple messages, events, a main sender/receiver and a main channel to communicate with them.
 
**Note**: A `Ticket` requires at least one [TicketMessage](#TicketMessage-object) in it's `messages` list.

sender.description: The user who created this ticket. Most of the time it will be the same as the sender, but not always: for example if an agent created the ticket on behalf of a user, the agent would be the sender when the user would be the requester.

closed_datetime.description: When the ticket was closed for the last time.

snooze_datetime.description: When the ticket will be automatically re-open. Set if an agent wants to come back to this ticket later

via.description: Indicates via which means this ticket was created. For example, a ticket of channel `email` could have been created through the API because of an integration; the `via` would then be `api`.

opened_datetime.description: When the ticket was opened for the first time.

updated_datetime.description: When the ticket was updated for the last time.

from_agent.description: Indicates whether the ticket was created by an agent or by a user.

channel.description: The channel used to communicate with the user on this ticket.

events.description: Events linked to this ticket. Currently only `action-executed` events are shown for performancereasons. If you need events such as `ticket-created`, `ticket-updated`, etc.. use the Events API.

items.description:
An `Event` is used to let you know if anything happened on your [Account](#Account-object).
For example: if a [user](#User-object) was created there will be an [Event](#Event-object) object with the 
type: "user-created".

Events are mostly used for internal purposes (ticket-created, ticket-updated, etc..), but you can create your own 
custom events and store them in Gorgias.

data.description: Raw JSON data of the event

type.description: Event types are used to discriminate between events.There are certain system-reserved event types.

user.description: The user that triggered this event or is somehow attached to it. If `None`, it's the account owner (Rules for example don't have a user).

messages.description: All the messages of the ticket.

spam.description: Indicates whether the ticket is considered as spam or not.

requester.description: The user who is requesting something in this ticket.

last_message_datetime.description: When a message was received or sent for the last time.

last_received_message_datetime.description: When a message was received for the last time.

language.description:
The [ISO-639-1](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/List_of_ISO_639-1_codes) language code of the client's messages on this ticket. 
This field is automatically generated by Gorgias if empty.

status.description: Ticket status is used for managing the lifecycle of the ticket

priority.description: Indicates the importance of the ticket.

tags.description: The tags associated with the ticket.

trashed_datetime.description: When the ticket was moved to the trash for the last time.

receiver.description: The original receiver of the ticket.

external_id.description: Another ID for the ticket. Can be set freely.

assignee_user.description: The agent assigned to this ticket.

Attachment.description:
An `Attachment` is an object that describes a file attachment to a
        [ticket message](#TicketMessage-object).
They can come from Emails, Chats, Facebook messages or can be supplied via the API. 
Other than the attachment URL it contains other useful metadata such as size, content type and the file name.

content_type.description: The MIME type of the file.

size.description: The size of the file, in bytes.

url.description: The URL to access to the attached file.

public.description: Whether or not the attachment can be accessed.

SourceAddress.description:
A `SourceAddress` is an individual address and name. `SourceAddress` is part of 
        the `Source` object.

name.description: Name of this address. Usually person's first + last name.

address.description: Actual address of the entry. Can be an email, facebook id, phone number, etc..

Tag.description:
A `Tag` (or label) is used to mark [Tickets](#Ticket-object) with a certain topic or category.
They are used in (Views)[#View-object], (Rules)[#Rule-object] and in Statistics.

For example you can have a 'Refund' ticket [View](#View-object) that displays all tickets that have `refund` tag which 
could then be reviewed by a manager at the end of the day.

usage_count.description: Number of tickets this tag is attached to.

name.description: Name of the tag. Keep it simple, a short 1 word tag is the best. The tag names are case sensitive.

updated_datetime.description: When the tag was last updated.

decoration.description: A JSON field that is used to decorate the tag with color and/or and icon.

UserChannel.description:
A `UserChannel` represents a way (an address) to communicate with a user. 
A [User](#User-object) can have multiple `UserChannel`.
For example a `User` can have multiple email addresses, phone numbers, etc.. All of these addresses are stored as an 
individual `UserChannel` object.

type.description: Channel type. Possible values: email, phone, chat, twitter, facebook-messenger, facebook-account, instagram

created_datetime.description: When the user channel was created.

updated_datetime.description: When the user channel was last updated.

preferred.description: Is this the preferred or (primary) channel to contact this user.

address.description: Address can be email, phone, facebook id, etc...

tags.description: Below you can find out how to create, update, delete, get and list your events as well as the description of the Event object itself.
tags.name: Event

tags.description: Below you can find out how to create, update, delete, get and list your tickets as well as the description of the Ticket object itself.
tags.name: Ticket

tags.description: Contains all the endpoints about how to manage users and their roles / groups / organisations.
tags.name: User

tags.description: Below you can find out how to create, update, delete, get and list your integrations as well as the description of the Integration object itself.
tags.name: Integration

tags.description: Below you can find out how to create, update, delete, get and list your rules as well as the description of the Rule object itself.
tags.name: Rule

tags.description: Contains all endpoints to manipulate views
tags.name: View

put.description: Update a user's fields, and return it's new version.

delete.description: Delete a user identified by the URL parameter {id}.

get.description: Retrieve a single user, identified by the URL parameter {id}. If {id} = 0, the current user is returned.

put.description: Update a Ticket identified by the URL parameter {id} and return it.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: the data of the Ticket you want to update

get.description: Retrieve a single Ticket, identified by the URL parameter {id}.
Get a widget based on {id}
Get users similar to passed one
get.summary: Get users similar to passed one

put.description: Update a TicketMessage and return it.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: the new data you want to set on the Message

delete.description: Delete a Message of a specific Ticket

get.description: Retrieve a single TicketMessage identified by the URL parameters {ticket_id} and {id}.

post.description: Create a new integration with a new or existing connection to an external service.

get.description: Get the list of all integrations (active or not) of the current account
Fetch a single Rule
Fetch all the rules of the current account
Get an event based on it's id.
Retrieve the list of Messages of a Ticket identified by the URL parameter {ticket_id}

put.description: Merge two tags together. The destination_tag is the one which will survive the merge, the source_tag is the one which will be discarded.

get.description: Get an event based on it's id.

put.description: When experimenting with views in the app sometimes it's useful to query something without saving a view. This endpoint allows you to do exactly that, apply an unsaved view to a list of tickets.

post.description: Create a new Ticket along with its Messages.

parameters.name: body
parameters.description: the data of the Ticket you want to create

get.description: Retrieve a list of tickets. If the {view} argument is present in the URL, then the list will correspond to this view's constraints.

ids.description: an array of ids of tickets you want to delete

put.description: Update an existing integration or its connection

delete.description: Delete an existing integration with the linked connection along

get.description: Fetch an integration's data with the linked connection.
Retrieve a single role, identified by the URL parameter {id}.

put.description: Merge two users according to the data sent in the payload. Replace all references to the second user's id with references to the first user's id.

post.description: Create a new user linked to the current user's account. It's role can be any combination of "user", "agent" and "admin".

get.description: Retrieve all users of the account of the current user, paginated.
Get all events of the account of the user, paginated.
Get all tickets of the specified user, sorted by DESC created_datetime.

post.description: Create a new role linked to the current user's account.

get.description: Retrieve all roles of the account of the current user, paginated.
Get all events of the specified user, sorted by DESC created_datetime.
Get all events of the account of the user, paginated.

put.description: Create/Update/Delete widgets of current account and passed context.

post.description: Create a new widget that will be displayed in the sidebar.

get.description: Get all widgets of the account of the user, paginated.

page.description: the number of the page you want to fetch
page.name: page

view_id.description: the id of the view which you want to filter the tickets
view_id.name: view_id

per_page.description: the number of tickets per page you're expecting
per_page.name: per_page
